name: Pride of Erin Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Quarter the cabbage, removing and discarding the core.
2. Cover with boiling water and let stand for five minutes.
3. Drain and pat dry. Shred the cabbage. Set aside.
4. In a heavy-bottomed pan over low heat, melt butter.
5. Simmer chopped onion until tender without browning.
6. Add shredded cabbage and potato to the pan. Stir gently.
7. Add ground mace. Stir in flour to coat all ingredients, but do not brown.
8. Gradually add liquids (milk and chicken boullion) to the mixture.
9. Bring to a boil and simmer for 20 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
10. Blend the soup until almost smooth, but still slightly lumpy.
11. Reheat the soup and season with salt and pepper to taste.
12. If the soup seems too thick, add a bit of hot milk.
13. Serve with a scoop of whipped cream and garnish with parsley and grated parmesan cheese on top.
